bitty documentation

Documentation

  • connection attributes [0005-bitty-connection]
    • data-connect [data-connect]
      Description
      • Each <bitty-8-0> requires a data-connect attribute.
      • The attribute defines the path to the JavaScript module to load.
      • Modules must contain an export const bitty = {} in order to work with bitty.
      • Multiple <bitty-8-0> tags can be placed on a single page.
      • Multiple modules can contain functions with the same name. Each will respond to incoming signals regardless of their source. (namespacing is on the roadmap to provide a seperation of concerns.)
      Examples
      • Make a connection to a module. [0010-basic-connection]
        HTML
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-connect/0010-basic-connection/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>

<div data-r="signal_B1D0F">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_B1D0F">Send B1D0F</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_B1D0F(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <!-- the bitty tag for this example is embedded directly in the example HTML -->
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-connect/0010-basic-connection/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_B1D0F]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_B1D0F]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Load multiple bitty modules on the same page. [0020-multiple-instances]
        HTML
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-connect/0020-multiple-instances/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>

<bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-connect/0020-multiple-instances/test_javascript_2.js"></bitty-8-0>

<div data-r="signal_A2EB2">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_A2EB2">Send A2EB2</button>

<div data-r="signal_A2EB2_2">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_A2EB2_2">Send A2EB2_2</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_A2EB2(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_A2EB2_2(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}
        Result
        waiting
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <!-- the bitty tags for this example is embedded directly in the example HTML -->
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-connect/0020-multiple-instances/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_A2EB2]").click();
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_A2EB2_2]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;

  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_A2EB2]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });

  const checkEls2 = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_A2EB2_2]");
  checkEls2.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • All modules with a signal function name respond to it. [0030-same-name]
        HTML
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-connect/0030-same-name/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>

<bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-connect/0030-same-name/test_javascript_2.js"></bitty-8-0>

<div data-r="signal_9E5B8_2" class="example">waiting</div>
<div data-r="signal_9E5B8_3" class="example">waiting</div>

<button data-s="signal_9E5B8">Send 9E5B8</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_9E5B8(ev, sender, el) {
  /************************************************
   * This function receives the initial
   * `signal_9E5B8` then triggers `signal_9E5B8_2`
   * which is unique to this module.
   ************************************************/
  bitty.trigger("signal_9E5B8_2");
}

export function signal_9E5B8_2(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_9E5B8(ev, sender, el) {
  /************************************************
   * This function receives the initial
   * `signal_9E5B8` then triggers `signal_9E5B8_3`
   * which is unique to this module.
   ************************************************/
  bitty.trigger("signal_9E5B8_3");
}

export function signal_9E5B8_3(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}
        Result
        waiting
        waiting
        Notes
        • [x] Each of these modules contains the same function named signal_9E5B8.
        • [x] When that signal is received, each one triggers another signal to an independent function (signal_9E5B8_2 in the first module and signal_9E5B8_3 in the second).
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <!-- the bitty tags for this example is embedded directly in the example HTML -->
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-connect/0030-same-name/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_9E5B8]").click();
  bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;

  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_9E5B8_2]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });

  const checkEls2 = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_9E5B8_3]");
  checkEls2.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • data-run [data-run]
      Description
      • data-run is an optional attribute that can be used on a bitty-8-0 tag.
      • If a data-run attribute exists it fires the signals from its value.
      • Multiple signals can be triggered from the data-run attribute.
      Examples
      • Run a function when the module has loaded. [0010-init-a-function]
        HTML
        <bitty-8-0 
  data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-run/0010-init-a-function/test_javascript.js" 
  data-run="signal_E34C9"></bitty-8-0>

<div data-r="signal_E34C9">waiting</div>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_E34C9(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <!-- the bitty tag for this example is embedded directly in the example HTML -->
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-run/0010-init-a-function/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_E34C9]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Fire multiple signals with `data-run`. [0020-init-multiple]
        HTML
        <bitty-8-0 
  data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-run/0020-init-multiple/test_javascript.js"
  data-run="signal_7B23F signal_7B23F_2"
></bitty-8-0>

<div data-r="signal_7B23F" class="example">waiting</div>
<div data-r="signal_7B23F_2" class="example">waiting</div>

export function signal_7B23F(ev, sender, el) {

}

  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}

        <!-- the bitty tag for this example is embedded directly in the example HTML -->

        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-run/0020-init-multiple/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest(_, __, ___) {
  bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_7B23F]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
  const checkEls2 = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_7B23F_2]");
  checkEls2.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
  • element attributes [0010-data-attributes]
    • data-listeners [data-listeners]
      Description
      • bitty listens for click and input events on data-s elements by default.
      • The data-listeners attribute can be used to change the events that an element listens for.
      • If a data-listeners attribute is used the element stops listening for the default click and input events.
      • The default click and input events can be added back in as part of the data-listeners to continue listening for them.
      Examples
      • Listen for a different type of event. [0010-custom-signal]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_6E38F" class="example">waiting</div>
<div 
  data-listeners="keydown" 
  data-s="signal_6E38F" 
  contenteditable="true"
  class="style_signal_6E38F"
></div>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_6E38F(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}
        Result
        waiting
        Notes
        • This example uses a keydown listener to allow automated testing (e.g. compared to a mouseover which is harder to automate)
        • bitty provides a mapKey() function that's likely to be of more use if you need to monitor key presses. Details on it are below.
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-listeners/0010-custom-signal/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-listeners/0010-custom-signal/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>

<style>
.style_signal_6E38F {
  background-color: var(--faded-info-color);
  border-radius: var(--default-border-radius);
  border: var(--accent-border);
  height: 3rem;
  padding: var(--default-padding);
  width: 10rem;
}
</style>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  await bitty.sleep(200);

  const event_signal_6E38F = new KeyboardEvent("keydown", {
    key: "a",
    bubbles: true,
    cancelable: true,
  });
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_6E38F]").dispatchEvent(event_signal_6E38F);

  // add to innerHTML as well for the visual update.
  // It doesn't affect the test. It helps avoid
  // confusion that would occur if there was
  // nothing in the div after the test was run.
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_6E38F]").innerHTML = "a";

  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_6E38F]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • The default event types (i.e. `click` and `input`) don't trigger if there's a `data-listeners` attribute exists on a `data-s` element and the value does not contain the default event types. [0020-defaults-removed]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_35510">won't change on click</div>
<button data-listeners="keydown" data-s="signal_35510">Click does nothing for 35510</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_35510(ev, sender, el) {
  // el.dataset.solo = true;
  el.innerHTML = "todo";
  // el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}
        Result
        won't change on click
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-listeners/0020-defaults-removed/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-listeners/0020-defaults-removed/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest(_, __, ___) {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_35510]").click();
  bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /won't change on click/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_35510]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • The default `click` and `input` event types can be added back into `data-listeners` to continue listening for them. [0030-defaults-added]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_7B421">waiting</div>
<button data-listeners="keydown click" data-s="signal_7B421">Send 7B421</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_7B421(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-listeners/0030-defaults-added/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-listeners/0030-defaults-added/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_7B421]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_7B421]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • data-r [data-r]
      Description
      Examples
      • Receive a single signal. [0010-basic-receive]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_E9FCA">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_E9FCA">Send E9FCA</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_E9FCA(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}

        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-r/0010-basic-receive/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-r/0010-basic-receive/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_E9FCA]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_E9FCA]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • data-s [data-s]
      Description
      • The data-s attribute defines what signals an element sends when an event it's associated with fires.
      • Multiple signals can be sent at the same time.
      • Signals sent that have no corresponding receivers trigger their related function once with a null value for the third/element argument.
      Examples
      • Send one signal to a single receiver. [0010-single-signal]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_B28CE">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_B28CE">Send B28CE</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_B28CE(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-s/0010-single-signal/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-s/0010-single-signal/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_B28CE]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_B28CE]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}

        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_294BF]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_294BF]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;

}
      • Send multiple signals to multiple receivers. [0020-multiple-signals]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_91921" class="example">waiting</div>
<div data-r="signal_91921_2" class="example">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_91921 signal_91921_2">
  Send 91921
</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_91921(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}

export function signal_91921_2(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}
        Result
        waiting
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-s/0020-multiple-signals/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-s/0020-multiple-signals/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest(_, __, ___) {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_91921").click();
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_91921_2").click();
  bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_91921]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
  const checkEls2 = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_91921_2]");
  checkEls2.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
  • bitty functions [0030-bitty-functions]
    • addListener(event, signals) [addListener]
      Description
      • [] Adds event listeners for a given event and sets it up to send the corresponding signals when it occurs.
      • [] Can be used to fire signals for events that aren't associated with an element.
      • [] Can be used to fire signals that could be associated with an element (e.g. you can add a click listener that listens for any click on the page)
      • [] Signal functions are called with the three arguments: the event, undefined, undefined)
      Examples
    • copyText(selector, separator = "") [copyText]
      Description
      • [] Copies the text from the given selector.
      • [] All elements that match the selector are used to populate the text (i.e. the selector is passed to querySelectorAll).
      • [] If multiple elements are found the string in the optional separator argument is used to join them.
      • [] If a selected element has a .value it's what's used for the text from the element.
      • [] If a selected element doesn't have a.value, then its .innerHTML is what's used for the text from the element.
      • [] Returns true if there was at least one element and the copy succeeded.
      • [] Returns false if the selector didn't match any elements.
      • [] Returns false if the copy failed.
      Examples
    • fetchData(url, options = {}); [fetchData]
      Description
      • [x] Fetches a JSON from a URL.
      • [x] Returns the JSON object if the fetch succeeds and the response parses as JSON.
      • [x] Returns undefined if either the fetch fails or the JSON fails to parse.
      • [x] Accepts and optional options object which is passed to the fetch call (e.g. for headers).
      • [x] Logs any errors to the console.
      Examples
      • Fetch and return a JSON object. [0010-basic-fetch]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_93E60">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_93E60">Send 93E60</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function signal_93E60(ev, sender, el) {
  const json = await bitty.fetchData("/versions/8/0/0/documentation/samples/valid-json.json");
  if (json.alfa === "bravo") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/fetchData/0010-basic-fetch/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/fetchData/0010-basic-fetch/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_93E60]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_93E60]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Return `undefined` if the fetch fails. [0020-fetch-fail-is-error]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_F3507">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_F3507">Send F3507</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function signal_F3507(ev, sender, el) {
  console.error(
    "NOTE: the 404 missing error for intentionally-missing-file.json is an expected error from testing.",
  );
  const response = await bitty.fetchData(
    "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/samples/intentionally-missing-file.json",
  );
  if (response === undefined) {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
  }
}

        view test harness

export async function runTestWithErrors() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_F3507]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_F3507]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Return `undefined` if JSON fails to parse. [0030-parse-fail-is-error]

      • Pass options to the fetch call. [0040-pass-options]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_C0F7C">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_C0F7C">Send C0F7C</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function signal_C0F7C(ev, sender, el) {
  const options = {
    method: "GET",
  };
  const json = await bitty.fetchData("/versions/8/0/0/documentation/samples/valid-json.json", options);
  if (json.alfa === "bravo") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
  }
}

        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/fetchData/0040-pass-options/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>

        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/fetchData/0040-pass-options/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_C0F7C]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_C0F7C]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • fetchHTML(url, options = {}) [fetchHTML]
      Description
      • [x] Fetches a URL to get templates from.
      • [x] Each <template> tag at the root of the file is converted into a document fragment.
      • [x] The templates are returned as an objects.
      • [x] The id attribute on each <template> is used as the key for the template's document fragment in the object.
      • [x] <template> elements without an id are ignored.
      • [x] Options can be passed to the fetch call with the optional section argument.
      Examples
      • fetchTemplates(url, options) [0010-get-templates]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_EEECD">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_EEECD">Send EEECD</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function signal_EEECD(ev, sender, el) {
  const templates = await bitty.fetchHTML(
    "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/samples/valid-templates/index.html",
  );
  const checkEl = templates.alfa.querySelector("div");
  if (checkEl.innerHTML = "alfa") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/fetchHTML/0010-get-templates/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/fetchHTML/0010-get-templates/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_EEECD]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_EEECD]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      Notes
      • The source template file used is /versions/8/0/0/documentation/samples/valid-templates/index.html
      • You'll have to view source on the page to see anything since the templates don't render by default.
    • fetchSVG(urls, options = {}) [fetchSVG]
      Description
      • [] Alias for fetchSVGs(urls, options)
      Examples
    • fetchSVGs(urls, options = {}) [fetchSVGs]
      Description
      Examples
    • loadData(key, fallback = null) [loadData]
      Description
      • [x] Loads JSON data from localStorage if it exists and returns it.
      • [x] Provides for an optional object fallback to use if there's nothing in storage with the given key.
      • [x] Returns undefined if the localStorage doesn't exist and there's no fallback.
      • [x] Returns undefined if the value in localStorage can't be parsed as JSON.
      Examples
      • Load existing JSON [0010-load-existing-json]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_E0326">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_E0326">Send E0326</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_E0326(ev, sender, el) {
  const json = bitty.loadData("E0326");
  if (json.alfa === "bravo") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/loadData/0010-load-existing-json/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 
  data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/loadData/0010-load-existing-json/test_verification_javascript.js"
  data-run="loadSampleDatasignal_E0326"
  ></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function loadSampleDatasignal_E0326() {
  bitty.saveData("E0326", { alfa: "bravo" });
}

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_E0326]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_E0326]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Use an optional object fallback if there is nothing in storage for the given key. [0020-object-fallback]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_FA2FA">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_FA2FA">Send FA2FA</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_FA2FA(ev, sender, el) {
  const json = bitty.loadData("no-storage-for-signal_FA2FA", {
    targetsignal_FA2FA: true,
  });
  if (json.targetsignal_FA2FA === true) {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
  }
}

        view test harness

  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_FA2FA]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Return `undefined` if there's not a value in storage for the key and there's no fallback. [0030-undefined-if-no-storage-or-fallback]

        export const bitty = {};

export function loadSampleDatasignal_1ED93() {
  localStorage.setItem("invalid_signal_1ED93", "not a valid json string");
}

export async function runTestWithErrors() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_1ED93]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_1ED93]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • localTimestamp(date = new Date()) [localTimestamp]
      Description
      • Outputs a timestamp in the format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS for the current locale.
      • The hour in HH is based of a 24 hour clock (e.g. 3PM is 15).
      • If a Date is passed to the function the output is based off it.
      • If no argument is provided the function uses new Date() for the time.
      Examples
      • Display the current time. [0010-current-time]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_DF095">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_DF095">Send DF095</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_DF095(ev, sender, el) {
  const timestamp = bitty.localTimestamp();
  el.innerHTML = timestamp;
}
        Result

        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example

        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/localTimestamp/0010-current-time/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_DF095]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_DF095]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Display a specific time. [0020-specific-time]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_99CE6">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_99CE6">Send 99CE6</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_99CE6(ev, sender, el) {
  const date = new Date(2026, 0, 1, 13, 45, 55);
  const timestamp = bitty.localTimestamp(date);
  el.innerHTML = timestamp;
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/localTimestamp/0020-specific-time/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/localTimestamp/0020-specific-time/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_99CE6]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_99CE6]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      Notes
      • The purpose of this function is to provide a concise display of a time for the current locale. It is not a valid ISO 8601, RFC 3339, or RFC 9557 timestamp.
    • localTimestampMS(date = new Date()) [localTimestampMS]
      Description
      • Operates the same as localTimestamp() but adds milliseconds to the end of the string.
      Examples
      • Display the current time with milliseconds. [0010-current-time]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_7CB08">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_7CB08">Send 7CB08</button>

        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_2DB48">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_2DB48">Send 2DB48</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_2DB48(ev, sender, el) {
  const date = new Date(2026, 0, 1, 13, 45, 55);
  const timestamp = bitty.localTimestampMs(date);
  el.innerHTML = timestamp;
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/localTimestampMS/0020-specific-time/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/localTimestampMS/0020-specific-time/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_2DB48]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_2DB48]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • mapKey(key, signals, modKeys = [], options = { listener: keydown, preventDefault: true}) [mapKey]
      Description
      • [x] Maps a key event to a signal signal.
      • [x] Maps a key event to multiple signals.
      • [x] Uses keydown by default.
      • [x] Listener can be set to keyup.
      • [x] Modifier keys can be defined (see modKeyAliases() for the list of keys).
      • [x] The event won't trigger if unknown modifier keys are specified.
      • [x] Prevents the browser from responding to the key press via preventDefault.
      • [x] preventDefault can be turned off.
      • [x] Key Codes can be used instead of strings by using a number instead of the character.
      Examples
      • Map a single key to a single signal [0010-map-single-to-single]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_07868">waiting</div>
<div>Press `q`</div>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function setKey_signal_07868() {
  /******************************************
   * This functions is run via the `data-run`
   * attribute in the <bitty-8-0> tag.
   ******************************************/
  bitty.mapKey("q", "signal_07868");
}

export function signal_07868(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}
        Result
        waiting
        Press `q`
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 
data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/mapKey/0010-map-single-to-single/test_javascript.js"
data-run="setKey_signal_07868"
></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/mapKey/0010-map-single-to-single/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const event_signal_6E38F = new KeyboardEvent("keydown", {
    key: "q",
    bubbles: true,
    cancelable: true,
  });
  document.dispatchEvent(event_signal_6E38F);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_07868]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Map a single key to multiple signals. [0020-map-single-to-multiple]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_CFB66">waiting</div>
<div data-r="signal_CFB66_2">waiting</div>

export function setKey() {
  /******************************************
   * This functions is run via the `data-run`
   * attribute in the <bitty-8-0> tag.
   ******************************************/
  bitty.mapKey("w", "signal_CFB66");
}

export function signal_CFB66(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}

        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const event_signal_6E38F = new KeyboardEvent("keydown", {
    key: "w",
    bubbles: true,
    cancelable: true,
  });
  document.dispatchEvent(event_signal_6E38F);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_CFB66]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Map a key with modifier keys (e.g. "shift", "alt", etc...) [0030-mod-keys]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_DE6CE">waiting</div>
<div>Press `alt + e`</div>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function setKey() {
  /******************************************
   * This functions is run via the `data-run`
   * attribute in the <bitty-8-0> tag.
   ******************************************/
  bitty.mapKey("e", "signal_DE6CE", ["altKey"]);
}

export function signal_DE6CE(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}

        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const event_signal_6E38F = new KeyboardEvent("keydown", {
    key: "e",
    altKey: true,
    bubbles: true,
    cancelable: true,
  });
  document.dispatchEvent(event_signal_6E38F);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_DE6CE]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Aliases (e.g. `ALT`) can be used in place of the explicit JavaScript event names (e.g. `altKey`). The full list of options is available in `modKeyAliases()`. [0035-mod-aliases]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_6B685">waiting</div>
<div>Press `alt + r`</div>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function setKey() {
  /******************************************
   * This functions is run via the `data-run`
   * attribute in the <bitty-8-0> tag.
   ******************************************/
  bitty.mapKey("r", "signal_6B685", ["ALT"]);
}

export function signal_6B685(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}

export async function runTest() {
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const event_signal_6E38F = new KeyboardEvent("keydown", {
    key: "r",
    altKey: true,
    bubbles: true,
    cancelable: true,
  });
  document.dispatchEvent(event_signal_6E38F);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_6B685]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Unknown modifier keys are not allowed. [0038-unknow-mods-fail]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_273EF" class="manual-test">there's no output from this example.</div>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function setKey_signal_273EF() {
  /******************************************
   * This function is called when the
   * test is run.
   ******************************************/
  bitty.mapKey("t", "signal_273EF", ["invalidModKey"]);
}

export function signal_273EF(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}
        Result
        there's no output from this example.
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example

        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTestWithErrors() {
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  bitty.trigger("setKey_signal_273EF");
  const event_signal_6E38F = new KeyboardEvent("keydown", {
    key: "t",
    bubbles: true,
    cancelable: true,
  });
  document.dispatchEvent(event_signal_6E38F);
  const pattern = /there's no output from this example./;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_273EF]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Allow default action to occur (i.e. don't apply `preventDefault()` to the event). [0050-allow-default]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_9EAED">waiting</div>
<div>Press `/`</div>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function setKey_signal_9EAED() {
  /******************************************
   * This functions is run via the `data-run`
   * attribute in the <bitty-8-0> tag.
   ******************************************/
  bitty.mapKey("/", "signal_9EAED", [], { preventDefault: false });
}

export function signal_9EAED(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}

export async function runTest() {
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const event_signal_6E38F = new KeyboardEvent("keydown", {
    key: "/",
    bubbles: true,
    cancelable: true,
  });
  document.dispatchEvent(event_signal_6E38F);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_9EAED]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Use `keyup` instead of `keydown` [0060-keyup]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_78314">waiting</div>
<div>Press `b`</div>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function setKey_signal_78314() {
  /******************************************
   * This functions is run via the `data-run`
   * attribute in the <bitty-8-0> tag.
   ******************************************/
  bitty.mapKey("b", "signal_78314", [], { listener: "keyup" });
}

export function signal_78314(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}

        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const event_signal_6E38F = new KeyboardEvent("keyup", {
    key: "b",
    bubbles: true,
    cancelable: true,
  });
  document.dispatchEvent(event_signal_6E38F);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_78314]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Use a key code instead number of a character string. [0080-key-codes]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_80715">waiting</div>
<div>Press `y`</div>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function setKey_signal_80715() {
  /******************************************
   * This functions is run via the `data-run`
   * attribute in the <bitty-8-0> tag.
   ******************************************/
  bitty.mapKey(89, "signal_80715");
}

export function signal_80715(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}

        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const event_signal_6E38F = new KeyboardEvent("keydown", {
    keyCode: 89,
    bubbles: true,
    cancelable: true,
  });
  document.dispatchEvent(event_signal_6E38F);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_80715]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      Notes
      • preventDefault() is applied unless otherwise specified.
      • If preventDefault() occurs for any key via mapKey() it affects all the other uses of that key (i.e. trying to use preventDefault() for a key in one place and not in another causes a race condition with undefined behaviour).
    • modKeyAlises() [modKeyAliases]
      Description
      • [x] Returns the object that holds the available aliases for modifier keys used with mapKey()
      • [x] The values sent to mapKey() are case insensitive (they always transformed to lowercase when doing the mapping).
      Examples
      • Get the aliases [0010-get-aliases]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_13C5D">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_13C5D">Send 13C5D</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_13C5D(_, __, el) {
  if (bitty.modKeyAliases().alt === "altKey") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/modKeyAliases/0010-get-aliases/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/modKeyAliases/0010-get-aliases/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_13C5D]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_13C5D]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • qs(selector) [qs]
      Description
      Examples
    • qsa(selector) [qsa]
      Description
      Examples
    • render(source, substitutions = {}) [render]
      Description
      • [x] Used to render a template with substitutions.
      • [x] Takes input from a Document Fragment.
      • [x] Takes input from an HTML Element.
      • [x] Takes input from a string.
      • [x] Always returns a Document Fragment.
      • [x] Applies substitutions from an optional object passed as the second argument.
      • [x] Substitution is done by converting the input into a string and doing find/replace on that string.
      • [x] Keys of the substitution object are the strings that are searched for.
      • [x] Values from the substitution object are what's used to replace the found strings.
      • [x] Replacements can be a string.
      • [x] Replacements can can be an array of strings which get joined directly together.
      • [x] Replacements can be an HTML Element.
      • [x] Replacements can be an array of HTML Elements.
      • [x] Replacements can be a Document Fragment.
      • [x] Replacements can be an array of Document Fragments.
      Examples
      • Use a document fragment for input. [0010-render-without-subs]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_E74C7">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_E74C7">Send E74C7</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

const input = document.createDocumentFragment();
const newEl = document.createElement("div");
newEl.innerHTML = "signal_E74C7";
input.appendChild(newEl);

export function signal_E74C7(ev, sender, el) {
  const output = bitty.render(input);
  const checkEl = output.firstChild;
  if (checkEl.innerHTML === "signal_E74C7") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0010-render-without-subs/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0010-render-without-subs/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_E74C7]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_E74C7]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Use a string for input. [0020-input-string]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_736E9">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_736E9">Send 736E9</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_736E9(ev, sender, el) {
  const html = bitty.render("<div>signal_736E9</div");
  const result = html.firstChild.innerHTML;
  if (result === "signal_736E9") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0020-input-string/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0020-input-string/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_736E9]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_736E9]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Use an HTML Element as input. [0025-element-input]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_4A624">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_4A624">Send 4A624</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

const input = document.createElement("div");
input.innerHTML = "signal_4A624";

export function signal_4A624(ev, sender, el) {
  const html = bitty.render(input);
  const result = html.firstChild.innerHTML;
  if (result === "signal_4A624") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0025-element-input/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0025-element-input/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_4A624]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_4A624]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Perform string substitutions. [0040-string-subs]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_B1061">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_B1061">Send B1061</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

const input = document.createDocumentFragment();
const newEl = document.createElement("div");
newEl.innerHTML = "TARGET_signal_B1061";
input.appendChild(newEl);

const subs = {
  "TARGET_signal_B1061": "UPDATED_signal_B1061",
};

export function signal_B1061(ev, sender, el) {
  const output = bitty.render(input, subs);
  const checkEl = output.firstChild;
  if (checkEl.innerHTML === "UPDATED_signal_B1061") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0040-string-subs/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>

        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0040-string-subs/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_B1061]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_B1061]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Use an array of strings for replacements. [0045-string-array]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_1AC47">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_1AC47">Send 1AC47</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

const input = document.createDocumentFragment();
const newEl = document.createElement("div");
newEl.innerHTML = "TARGET_signal_1AC47";
input.appendChild(newEl);

const subs = {
  "TARGET_signal_1AC47": ["UPDATED_", "signal_1AC47"],
};

export function signal_1AC47(ev, sender, el) {
  const output = bitty.render(input, subs);
  const checkEl = output.firstChild;
  if (checkEl.innerHTML === "UPDATED_signal_1AC47") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
  }
}

        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_1AC47]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_1AC47]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Use an HTML Element for the replacement. [0050-element-sub]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_F3DE4">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_F3DE4">Send F3DE4</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

const input = document.createDocumentFragment();
const newEl = document.createElement("div");
newEl.innerHTML = "TARGET_signal_F3DE4";
input.appendChild(newEl);

const subEl = document.createElement("div");
subEl.innerHTML = "UPDATED_signal_F3DE4";

const subs = {
  "TARGET_signal_F3DE4": subEl,
};

export function signal_F3DE4(ev, sender, el) {
  const output = bitty.render(input, subs);
  const checkEl = output.firstChild;
  if (checkEl.firstChild.innerHTML === "UPDATED_signal_F3DE4") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
  }
}

        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_F3DE4]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_F3DE4]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Use an array of HTML Elements as the replacement payload. [0055-element-array]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_02C95">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_02C95">Send 02C95</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

const input = document.createDocumentFragment();
const newEl = document.createElement("div");
newEl.innerHTML = "TARGET_signal_02C95";
input.appendChild(newEl);

const subEl1 = document.createElement("div");
subEl1.innerHTML = "UPDATED";
const subEl2 = document.createElement("div");
subEl2.innerHTML = "signal_02C95";

const subs = {
  "TARGET_signal_02C95": [subEl1, subEl2],
};

export function signal_02C95(ev, sender, el) {
  const output = bitty.render(input, subs);
  if (output.firstChild.children[1].innerHTML === "signal_02C95") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0055-element-array/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>

        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0055-element-array/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_02C95]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_02C95]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Use a Document Fragment for a replacement. [0060-frag-sub]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_1EDC7">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_1EDC7">Send 1EDC7</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

const input = document.createDocumentFragment();
const newEl = document.createElement("div");
newEl.innerHTML = "TARGET_signal_1EDC7";
input.appendChild(newEl);

const subFragment = document.createElement("template");
subFragment.innerHTML = "<div>UPDATED_signal_1EDC7</div>";

const subs = {
  "TARGET_signal_1EDC7": subFragment.content,
};

export function signal_1EDC7(ev, sender, el) {
  const output = bitty.render(input, subs);
  if (output.firstChild.firstChild.innerHTML === "UPDATED_signal_1EDC7") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0060-frag-sub/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0060-frag-sub/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_1EDC7]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_1EDC7]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Use an array of document fragments for as a replacement value. [0065-frag-array]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_B15F2">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_B15F2">Send B15F2</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

const input = document.createDocumentFragment();
const newEl = document.createElement("div");
newEl.innerHTML = "TARGET_signal_B15F2";
input.appendChild(newEl);

const subFragment1 = document.createElement("template");
subFragment1.innerHTML = "<div>UPDATED</div>";

const subFragment2 = document.createElement("template");
subFragment2.innerHTML = "<div>signal_B15F2</div>";

const subs = {
  "TARGET_signal_B15F2": [subFragment1.content, subFragment2.content],
};

export function signal_B15F2(ev, sender, el) {
  const output = bitty.render(input, subs);
  const result = output.firstChild.children[1].innerHTML;
  if (result === "signal_B15F2") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0065-frag-array/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>

        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0065-frag-array/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_B15F2]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_B15F2]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}

    • saveData(key, data) [saveData]

      Examples
      • saveJSON(key, data) [0010-save-json]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_393D6">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_393D6">Send 393D6</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_393D6(ev, sender, el) {
  const result = bitty.saveData("393D6", {});
  el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/saveData/0010-save-json/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>

        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/saveData/0010-save-json/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_393D6]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_393D6]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • send(payload, signal) [send]
      Description
      Examples
    • setCSS(key, value) [setCSS]
      Description
      Examples
    • sleep(ms) [sleep]
      Description
      Examples
    • sort(a, b) [sort]
      Description
      Examples
    • tee(split, input) [tee]
      Description
      Examples
    • trigger(signals) [trigger]
      Description
      Examples
    • uuid(dashes = true) [uuid]
      Description
      • Generates a random UUID with randomUUID()
      • Passing false for the optional argument removes the dashes from the UUID.
      Examples
  • page templates [0035-page-templates]
    • auto loaded page templates [page-templates]
      Description
      Examples
      • Use an auto loaded template. [0010-load-page-template]
        HTML
        <template id="template_signal_A662C">
  <div>Content signal_A662C</div>
</template>

<div data-r="signal_A662C">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_A662C">Send A662C</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_A662C(ev, sender, el) {
  const result =
    bitty.templates.template_signal_A662C.querySelector("div").innerHTML;
  if (result === "Content signal_A662C") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.localTimestamp();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0035-page-templates/page-templates/0010-load-page-template/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0035-page-templates/page-templates/0010-load-page-template/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_A662C]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_A662C]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
